rulings.Case Title: DANIEL PIERINI VS. COSMOPOLITAN PRIVATE SECURITY, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Daniel Pierini'S Notice Of Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment (From 12-1)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 5, 2011, Line 15, Plaintiff Daniel Pierini'S Notice Of Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Judgment. Denied. Code of Civil Procedure 473(b) is inapplicable and, even if it was applicable, the evidentiary showing made is not sufficient to warrant relief from a dismissal due to failure to prosecute. The motion is, in legal effect, one for reconsideration, but, even if so characterized, it fails to meet the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure 1008 since it is untimely and not supported by new law, facts or circumstances. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: FIRE INSURANCE EXCHANGE et al VS. JALILIE ELECTRIC, INC. DBA RIDGEWAY ELECTRIC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene As A Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 5, 2011, Line 5, OTHER USAA INSURANCE COMPANY's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Intervene As A Plaintiff. Granted. Proposed intervenor meets the standards for mandatory intervention and, even if it did not, the court exercises its discretion to grant permissive intervention. Judicial economy, identity of the issues, and the lack of legal prejudice to defendant all strongly favor intervention rather than a separate action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ANGELICA M. GONZALEZ VS. ALFONSO PICAZO, AS TRUSTEES OF THE ARTHUR AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Compel The Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents By Defts, Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 5, 2011, Line PLAINTIFF ANGELICA GONZALEZ' Hearing On Motion To Compel The Attendance At Deposition And Production Of Documents By Defts, Production Of Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions IS Granted. Both depositions are to be completed prior to December 13, 2011. The dates set forth in plaintiff¿s reply memorandum are reasonable. Although normally counsel should be able to schedule depositions without need for a motion, here neither side acted without substantial justification and thus all requests for sanctions are denied. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm today stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA BROOKS VS. BAE SYSTEMS INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Add Newly Filed Case To Consolidated Action
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 5, 2011, Line 12, PLAINTIFF BARBARA BROOKS' Motion To Add Newly Filed Case To Consolidated Action. Granted without prejudice to seeking severance of this case from the eight other cases from either a single assignment judge or the trial judge. This case is added to and consolidated for all purposes with the eight cases referred to in order filed May 18, 2011. The lead case remains 504947. Common issues of law and fact and judicial economy support adding this case to the eight other cases. rulings.Case Title: KATHLEEN STACK VS. KENDRA STACK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Order To Disqualify Pltf'S Counsel And All Attorneys Associated With Him
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 5, 2011, Line 13, DEFENDANT KENDRA STACK's Notice Of Motion For Order To Disqualify Plaintiff'S Counsel And All Attorneys Associated With Him. Denied. No attorney-client relationship was ever established between defendant and Mr. Davis or his office. However, even if there was some fiduciary duty or expectation of confidentiality that arose by virtue of Mr. Davis¿s procuring the hard money loan, defendant has not shown that she provided any confidential information to Mr. Davis. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
